content.full_text:
February 12, 1990

Kathryn E. Donovan, General Counsel
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Intermediary Reporting

We request advice concerning campaign provisions regarding the following hypothetical situation:

An individual goes out and collects three contributions of $75.00 each from three completely separate contributors (none of the three had given before). The sum of the three checks equals $225.00. Does this individual need to be reported as an intermediary due to the fact that the amount collected is over the reporting threshold of $100.00? Even though none of the contributions need to be reported.

Please advise in writing as soon as possible.

Thank you.

Sincerely,

CLENDENIN, BIRD & BILLINGTON

Constance H. Bird
Certified Public Accountant
